payload.title: MTA 2026 Adopted Budget, February Financial Plan 2026-2029
payload.description: MTA 2026 Adopted Budget and February Financial Plan 2026-2029 including consolidated financial statements, subsidy schedules, debt service, capital program information, and agency financial plans.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Primary operating agency within the Metropolitan Transportation Authority responsible for subway and bus operations in New York City.
Also known as the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (TBTA).

tool_args.label: Total Expenses with Debt Service 2026 Adopted Budget

payload.description: Extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station, creating four new accessible stations in the Bronx (Hunts Point, Parkchester, Morris Park and Co-Op City), improving existing tracks and bridges and cutting travel times from the Bronx to Manhattan.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Project: Penn Station Access. This project will create an extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station, creating four new accessible stations, improve existing tracks and bridges and cut travel times from the Bronx to Manhattan by as much as 50 minutes
Bronx: Hunts Point, Parkchester, Morris Park and Co-Op City
Full completion of the project, originally scheduled for 2027, is now expected to be delayed until 2030.
net impact on the operating budget is initially estimated to be $55 million

warnings: project.operator is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Operator is an entity relation, not a project enum. Suggested relation: operated_by.

payload.claim_text: MTA is planning to begin providing about 30 percent of the planned scheduled service to three of the four new Bronx stations in 2027.
payload.subject: Penn Station Access

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA is planning to begin providing about 30 percent of the planned scheduled service to three of the four new Bronx stations in 2027.

payload.claim_text: Savings targets from new operating efficiencies: $75 million in 2026, growing to $150 million in 2027 and $200 million in 2028, reaching $250 million in annual recurring operating savings by 2029.
payload.subject: MTA Operating Efficiencies

evidence_refs.source_quote: Achievable savings of $75 million in 2026, growing to $150 million in 2027 and $200 million in 2028, are expected to reach a full value of $250 million in annual recurring operating savings by 2029.

payload.description: Making subway stations accessible to customers with disabilities per a 2022 legal settlement, including new elevator and/or ramp installations and other accessibility features.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Project: Station Accessibility (ADA). As agreed to in a 2022 legal settlement, the MTA is committed to making subway stations accessible to customers with disabilities.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.description: Signal modernization projects replacing legacy conventional signaling systems with new CBTC signaling systems. Third-party contractual needs estimated at $23 million to $28 million annually starting in 2026.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Project: Communication Based Train Control Projects (CBTC). CBTC projects are signal modernization projects that replace old legacy conventional signaling systems with new CBTC signaling systems.

tool_args.label: Bridges and Tunnels Capital Maintenance Projects

payload.project_name: Bridges and Tunnels Capital Maintenance Projects
payload.description: Multiple capital projects including Main Cable Dehumidification at Bronx-Whitestone Bridge, Throgs Neck Bridge, and Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge; and Installation of Fire Suppression System at Hugh L. Carey Tunnel and Queens-Midtown Tunnel.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Project: Main Cable Dehumidification at Bronx-Whitestone Bridge & Throgs Neck Bridge
Project: Main Cable Dehumidification at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
Project: Installation of Fire Suppression System at the Hugh L. Carey Tunnel and the Queens-Midtown Tunnel

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

payload.description: Transition to a fully zero emission fleet using all-electric buses, impacting maintenance of bus charging infrastructure, training, and electric power expenses for NYCT and MTA Bus.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA announced a commitment to transition to a fully zero emission fleet using all-electric buses.

payload.description: Making subway stations accessible to customers with disabilities per a 2022 legal settlement, including new elevator and/or ramp installations and other accessibility features.

evidence_refs.source_quote: As agreed to in a 2022 legal settlement, the MTA is committed to making subway stations accessible to customers with disabilities, consistent with the Americans with Disabilities Act.

tool_args.label: Fare and Toll Rate Increases Proposed

payload.claim_text: Effective March 2027 and March 2029, biennial fare and toll rate changes to generate a 4 percent yield increase in farebox and toll revenues, projected to generate $1.22 billion over the Plan period.
payload.subject: fares and tolls

evidence_refs.source_quote: this Plan proposes, effective March 2027 and March 2029, biennial fare and toll rate changes to generate a 4 percent yield increase in farebox and toll revenues.

payload.claim_text: FEMA reimbursement of direct COVID-related expenses expected over the 2026 to 2028 period, with receipt of $200 million each year.
payload.subject: FEMA COVID reimbursement

evidence_refs.source_quote: FEMA reimbursement is expected over the 2026 to 2028 period, with receipt of $200 million each year.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Casino License and Gaming Tax Revenue Assumption

payload.claim_text: Plan assumes casino license and gaming tax revenues of $500 million in both 2026 and 2027, $600 million in 2028, and $200 million in 2029.
payload.subject: casino revenue

evidence_refs.source_quote: Casino revenue. The awarding of casino licenses is expected by the end of 2025.

payload.claim_text: The February Plan continues to present a balanced budget annually through 2026.
payload.subject: MTA budget

evidence_refs.source_quote: The February Plan continues to present a balanced budget annually through 2026.

payload.description: The public benefit corporation responsible for public transportation in the New York State metropolitan area.
The MTA 2026 Adopted Budget is published by the Metropolitan Transportation Authority.
New York City Transit is a constituent operating agency of the MTA.
Long Island Rail Road is a constituent operating agency of the MTA.
Metro-North Railroad is a constituent operating agency of the MTA.
